categories.description: block_on() calls on the UI/event-loop thread that freeze the interface while async I/O completes

items.description: block_on(quicklinks_storage.load_all()) inside handle_open_quick_links — blocks UI while loading quicklinks from SQLite when re-activating existing window
items.suggested_fix: Spawn a background task via cx.background_executor() to load links, then update the view in a callback. Use a loading spinner placeholder.

items.description: runtime.block_on(storage.load_all()) inside handle_open_quick_links — blocks UI while loading quicklinks when creating a new window
items.suggested_fix: Same as UIB-1: load asynchronously, show empty view first, then populate.

items.description: block_on(quicklinks_storage.load_all()) inside handle_manage_quicklinks — blocks UI thread when opening manage quicklinks
items.suggested_fix: Move to background executor, update view on completion.

items.description: block_on(quicklinks_storage.load_all()) inside handle_browse_quicklink_library — blocks UI thread when browsing library
items.suggested_fix: Move to background executor, update view on completion.

items.description: block_on(storage.load_all()) inside handle_execute_quicklink — blocks UI while loading all quicklinks just to find one by ID
items.suggested_fix: Add a storage.get_by_id() method, or load all asynchronously. This call is particularly wasteful since it loads all links to find one.

categories.description: SearchConfig.timeout field (100ms default) is defined but never enforced — slow providers can block indefinitely

items.description: SearchConfig.timeout is set to Duration::from_millis(100) but neither search_sync() nor search()/search_with_cancellation() wrap provider calls in a timeout. search_sync() calls each provider sequentially with no time limit. search() uses tokio::task::spawn_blocking without tokio::time::timeout wrapping.
items.suggested_fix: In search_with_cancellation(), wrap each spawn_blocking handle with tokio::time::timeout(self.config.timeout, handle). In search_sync(), check elapsed time after each provider and bail early. For the async path: `match tokio::time::timeout(self.config.timeout, handle).await { Ok(Ok(results)) => ..., _ => vec![] }`

categories.description: Tests with inherent race conditions due to background thread timing

items.description: test_prefetcher_trigger_starts_running — asserts status reaches Running within 5s, but the background thread may complete so fast it jumps to Completed before the poll loop observes Running. The wait_for_status helper handles this (>= comparison), but on slow CI the 50ms initial_delay plus Spotlight query can vary wildly.
items.suggested_fix: Accept both Running and Completed as success in the assertion (already partially done). Alternatively, use a barrier or condvar to synchronize instead of polling.

items.description: test_start_background_prefetch — creates a real SpotlightSearchService and triggers actual Spotlight queries. Flaky in CI (no Spotlight index), timing-dependent assertions on status reaching Running/Completed within 5–10s.
items.suggested_fix: Mock SpotlightSearchService or use a trait-based abstraction. For now, mark #[ignore] in CI or gate with env var. The real fix is injecting a mock service that returns canned results.

categories.description: Calls to .unwrap() outside #[cfg(test)] modules that could panic in production

items.description: cache.lock().unwrap() — std::sync::Mutex poisoning panic if another thread panicked while holding the lock
items.suggested_fix: Use parking_lot::Mutex (no poisoning) or handle the PoisonError: cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())

items.description: cache.lock().unwrap() — same std::sync::Mutex poisoning risk
items.suggested_fix: Same as UNW-1.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — compile-time constant regex pattern
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal regex in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — compile-time constant regex
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal regex in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — compile-time constant regex
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal regex in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — 6 compile-time constant regex patterns for calculator parsing
items.suggested_fix: Safe — all literal regex strings in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() inside is_time_format() function — called on every input evaluation
items.suggested_fix: Safe pattern, but wasteful — regex is recompiled on each call. Move to a LazyLock static for efficiency.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — compile-time constant regex patterns for implicit multiplication
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal regex in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in LazyLock — 4 compile-time constant timer parsing patterns
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal regex in LazyLock. No change needed.

items.description: self.event_store.as_ref().unwrap() — panics if event_store is None, but called immediately after setting it in a conditional block above
items.suggested_fix: The code checks is_none() and sets it, then unwraps. While logically safe, refactor to use get_or_insert_with() pattern: `self.event_store.get_or_insert_with(|| unsafe { EKEventStore::new() })`

items.description: input.chars().next().unwrap() — called after checking !input.is_empty() on line 160, so logically safe
items.suggested_fix: Logically safe due to prior empty check. Could use .expect("checked non-empty") for documentation.

items.description: NonZeroUsize::new(50).unwrap() — literal non-zero value
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal 50 is non-zero. No change needed.

items.description: self.builders.into_iter().next().unwrap() — called only when builders.len() == 1 (checked on line 925)
items.suggested_fix: Safe due to prior length check. Could use .expect("checked len == 1") for clarity.

items.description: NonZeroUsize::new(1).unwrap() used as fallback in .unwrap_or() — literal non-zero
items.suggested_fix: Safe — literal 1 is non-zero. Could use NonZeroUsize::MIN for cleaner code.

items.description: TODO: Handle multi-line code blocks — extension preview markdown rendering incomplete

items.description: TODO: Implement UI commands for: — entire module appears to be a stub for planned app commands

categories.description: Command::new().spawn() calls where the Child handle is dropped without waiting, potentially leaving zombie processes

items.description: Three spawn() calls for pmset sleepnow, pmset displaysleepnow, and open ScreenSaverEngine. The Child handle is returned from spawn but the Result is propagated with ? — the Child is then dropped, meaning the process is detached. For fire-and-forget commands (sleep, screensaver) this is acceptable.
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE for fire-and-forget system commands. Optionally spawn a thread to wait() to avoid zombie PIDs.

items.description: Command::new('open').spawn()? for opening System Preferences — Child dropped immediately
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE — opening System Preferences is fire-and-forget.

items.description: let _ = Command::new('open').arg(&path).spawn() — explicitly discards result and Child handle when opening a file
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE for 'open' command. The let _ = pattern explicitly acknowledges the dropped handle.

items.description: Multiple let _ = Command::new('open').spawn() calls for opening URLs, apps, and paths
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE for fire-and-forget 'open' invocations.

items.description: let _ = Command::new('qlmanage').spawn() — Quick Look preview, Child dropped
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE, but qlmanage should ideally be killed when the preview is dismissed. Consider storing the Child handle.

items.description: let _ = Command::new('open').spawn() — opening calendar event URL, Child dropped
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE for fire-and-forget URL opening.

items.description: command.spawn()? — Child is stored in SandboxedExtension struct, properly managed. NOT an orphan.
items.suggested_fix: NO FIX NEEDED — process handle is stored and managed.

items.description: Command::new('qlmanage').spawn() — Quick Look preview for app info, Child dropped
items.suggested_fix: Store the Child handle so the qlmanage process can be killed when the preview panel is closed.

items.description: Command::new('osascript').spawn() for window focus — fire-and-forget AppleScript
items.suggested_fix: ACCEPTABLE for one-shot osascript commands.

categories.description: The 1 known clippy warning in photoncast-apps

items.description: merge_app_config function — clippy warning: 'this argument is passed by value, but not consumed in the function body' at line 193. The `incoming: AutoQuitAppConfig` parameter is passed by value but only its fields are read (enabled, timeout_minutes) or moved (last_active). Clippy detects that the struct itself isn't consumed as a whole.
items.suggested_fix: Change signature to `incoming: &AutoQuitAppConfig` and adjust field access: use `incoming.enabled` (Copy), `incoming.timeout_minutes` (Copy), and `incoming.last_active` (clone or borrow). Alternatively, since last_active is an Option<DateTime> (which is Copy via chrono), simply changing to `&AutoQuitAppConfig` should work directly.
